entry.title: Re: Open Item Management for AUC Accounts (Fixed Asset –...
entry.content:
Hello @SandraJove 

Thank you for your question.

No, the standard approach is not to manage AUC G/L accounts like vendor open item accounts. The usual control point for AUC is the asset, the investment project/WBS, and the settlement chain, not open item management on the G/L account itself. In Public Cloud, Journal Entry Tracing for Settlement is the standard way to trace postings from the completed asset back to the source project and AUC.

There are two important design distinction points to note:

  1. Vendor style clearing is not the standard AUC pattern. Open item management is a G/L clearing concept for uncleared items. Your requirement is closer to construction in progress tracking and settlement reconciliation, which in SAP, is handled through the asset and project settlement flow, not through open item managed AUC balance sheet accounts.

    See the following references for more details.

    https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/fe27b4c6a02546d98bece37b89712d00.html?locale=en-US

    https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/3a83ebc6cec44a528444df0558465889.html?locale=en-US  

  2. If you need granular traceability, the recommended standard route is project-based AUC with settlement tracing.

    Here are references for more guidance

    https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/d5213bfdc97c41c49e136a52c38a975a.html?locale=en-US

    https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/3a83ebc6cec44a528444df0558465889.html?locale=en-US

The Public Cloud highlights in KBA 3443645 that creation and settlement of independent AuC is not supported in that scenario. So, if your business is thinking in terms of an old-style independent AUC open item style tracking, that is not the direction SAP chose in Public Cloud. The standard operational direction is the investment project/WBS/settlement pattern.

Instead of asking “which G/L line is still open”, the question becomes:

  • Which project/WBS costs are still collected and not yet settled,
  • Which amounts have already moved to AUC,
  • Which amounts have already been capitalized to final asset, and
  • What is the remaining balance by AUC asset.

That gives you an auditable reconciliation path without you trying to force open item management on th AUC G/L.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Your Sherlock Homes – How to Segregate Configuration from Releasing Transports
entry.content:
Co-Author: Lisa Kouch

Introduction

Recently I got a question from a customer: In the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, we usually give BPC Expert business role to a user who does the CBC and system configurations. However, to segregate the responsibility, we don’t want that configuration user to be able to release transports; we want to add a layer of control for the internal control purpose.  Only the transport releaser can release a transport. How can I achieve this?

If the purpose of this responsibility lies in the approval and documentation purpose (aka process control), my blog Using SAP CALM Features App to Track Transports in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition should serve the purpose.  However, if you want to know the details on controlling this responsibility in the system, this blog discusses the segregation of configurations from releasing transports.

Check Out the Business Catalogs

If you are not familiar with the user management concept in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, please refer to my blog User Management in a Nutshell for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition.

As always, my first step of research is the infamous SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library.  Since I want to control who can release the configuration transports, let me check out the app Export Customizing Transports.  By selecting the IMPLEMENTATION INFORMATION tab, then expanding the Configuration section, we can find the important information as below:

Access Control of App Export Customizing Transports

There are three Business Catalogs control the access (disregard two deprecated ones):

  • SAP_CORE_BC_BCT_TRN_DSP_PC for Transport Display
  • SAP_CORE_BC_BCT_TRN_MNG_PC for Transport Management
  • SAP_CORE_BC_BCT_TRN_REL_PC for Transport Release Management

My idea is to only give the Configurator the Business Catalogs SAP_CORE_BC_BCT_TRN_DSP_PC and SAP_CORE_BC_BCT_TRN_MNG_PC so that he/she cannot release the transports. If you take out the SAP_CORE_BC_BCT_TRN_MNG_PC, the configurator cannot create a transport which is not what we want.

Next, I found out two Business Roles might contain these three business catalogs:

  • SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR
  • SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT

To verify my idea, I need to get into the system to check the Business Role derived from the template SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT.  In my case, it is the Z_BR_BPC_EXPERT.  All Three transport related business catalogs are there.

Three Transport Related Business Catalogs in the Business Role Z_BR_BPC_EXPERT

Testing

When all three business catalogs were assigned to the business role Z_BR_BPC_EXPERT, I created a dummy (empty) configuration transport.  Then I take out the business catalog SAP_CORE_BC_BCT_TRN_REL_PC.  When opening the app Export Customizing Transports, the Release button is grayed out. Therefore, I achieved the objective: the configuration user can view but cannot release the transport.  Furthermore, since the business catalog SAP_CORE_BC_BCT_TRN_MNG_PC is assigned, the user can still manage the transport, such as creating a transport, assigning a transport to him/herself, delete a transport, etc.

Without Business Catalog SAP_CORE_BC_BCT_TRN_REL_PC, the User Cannot Release a Transport

Recommendations

To handle the situation described in this blog, I recommend using an adapted business role from an SAP template. You can add or remove necessary business catalogs as needed.  For the transport releaser, you won’t give him/her the BPC Expert business role.  You can create a new role with very limited business catalog such as the SAP_CORE_BC_BCT_TRN_REL_PC and assign to him/her.  For the configurator, you should remove the business catalog SAP_CORE_BC_BCT_TRN_REL_PC from any business roles assigned to him/her.  In particular, we know that the business role derived from the SAP Template SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR includes the business catalog SAP_CORE_BC_BCT_TRN_REL_PC as shown below.  Therefore, don’t give the system configurator the administration business role.  Just be thorough when doing your investigation.

Two Transports Related Business Catalogs in the SAP Template SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR

Conclusion

By removing one business catalog SAP_CORE_BC_BCT_TRN_REL_PC usually assigned to the configurators as a BPC Expert, you can segregate the configuration from transport releases.

References

  • Blog: Using SAP CALM Features App to Track Transports in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Blog: User Management in a Nutshell for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition

entry.title: From Legacy ERP to SAP S/4HANA: Why Quality Governance is the Secret Sauce
entry.content:
After nearly two decades in the ERP landscape, witnessing the evolution from traditional legacy systems to the modern SAP S/4HANA, one fundamental truth remains: Technology evolves, but Business Logic is constant.

The move to S/4HANA is often treated as a purely technical migration. Projects focus heavily on "Go-Live" dates and system performance, but the real differentiator between a seamless transition and a post-migration crisis is often overlooked: Quality Governance.

Why does Quality Governance matter more in S/4HANA?

  1. The Ripple Effect of Integration: In S/4HANA, modules are more interconnected than ever. A minor discrepancy in Material Management (MM) or Sales (SD) ripples instantly through Financial Accounting (FI). Quality Governance isn't just about finding bugs; it’s about protecting the "Universal Journal" from corrupted data flows.
  2. Data Integrity vs. Migration Speed: The pressure to meet deadlines often turns UAT (User Acceptance Testing) into a "box-ticking" exercise. However, a governance-led approach ensures that testing validates the Financial Integrity of the data, not just the functionality of the buttons.
  3. The Bridge to Business Reality: Quality Governance serves as the translator between technical configuration and business requirements. It ensures that what is built actually supports the accounting standards and operational needs of the organization.

In the era of Cloud and AI-driven ERPs, Accuracy is the only currency that matters. Speed is important, but if the data isn't governed by quality from day one, the "Digital Transformation" stays on the surface.

I’d love to hear from the community: During your S/4HANA journey, was Quality Governance treated as a strategic pillar or just a final phase before Go-Live?

#SAP #S4HANA #QualityGovernance #ERP #Testing #DigitalTransformation #SAPFinance

References & Further Reading:

  • SAP Activate: https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/

  • McKinsey (Digital Transformation): https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/mckinsey-digital/our-insights/the-keys-to-a-successful-digital-transformation

  • ASUG S/4HANA Research: https://www.asug.com/insights

entry.title: Somos Comunidade SAP Curitiba – Iron Aparecido Lopes
entry.content:
Este post faz parte da série “Somos SAP Community Curitiba”, criada para compartilhar histórias de pessoas que ajudam a construir e fortalecer o ecossistema SAP em nossa cidade.

A ideia da série foi apresentada neste post de abertura: 🎙️ Série da comunidade: Somos SAP Community Curit... - SAP Community

A proposta é simples: responder algumas perguntas e contar um pouco da nossa jornada dentro da comunidade.

Fui convidado por @Fabiele para participar da série e compartilhar um pouco da minha jornada no ecossistema SAP. Você pode conferir o post dela aqui: Somos Comunidade SAP Curitiba – Fabiele Boico - SAP Community

 

👤Quem sou eu na SAP Community Curitiba?

 

Sou Iron Aparecido Lopes, consultor SAP, com foco principal no módulo PP, e atualmente também dedicado ao aprendizado do SAP SD. Estudo e pratico SAP nos ambientes ECC, S/4HANA e Cloud, buscando entender os processos de forma integrada, do negócio à execução no sistema.

Faço parte da CVA Consultoria, empresa onde tenho aprendido diariamente, tanto na parte técnica quanto na forma de enxergar projetos SAP de maneira estruturada e prática. Ter esse ambiente de aprendizado, aliado à troca com outros profissionais, tem sido fundamental na minha evolução.

Também tive a oportunidade de participar do SAP Inside Track Curitiba 2025, junto com o Time CVA, o que reforçou ainda mais meu interesse em estar presente, aprender e me conectar com a comunidade local.

 

 

🔥O que mais me motiva a continuar fazendo parte da SAP Community Curitiba?

 

O que mais me motiva é a troca aberta de conhecimento. A comunidade mostra, na prática, que aprender SAP não precisa ser um caminho solitário. Sempre existe alguém disposto a compartilhar uma experiência, uma dica ou até um erro que já viveu, e isso acelera muito o aprendizado de quem está começando.

Além disso, ver profissionais de diferentes níveis participando ativamente, seja em eventos, posts ou conversas, cria um ambiente seguro para aprender, perguntar e evoluir.

 

 

🚀Como fazer parte da comunidade impactou minha jornada profissional ou pessoal?

 

A SAP Community Curitiba tem sido importante principalmente como direcionamento e inspiração. Acompanhar histórias reais, eventos como o SAP Inside Track Curitiba e a participação ativa das pessoas da comunidade ajuda a entender melhor o ecossistema SAP e onde é possível chegar com dedicação e estudo.

Mais do que aprendizado técnico, a comunidade reforça algo essencial: ninguém se desenvolve sozinho. Esse sentimento de pertencimento e de aprendizado coletivo faz toda a diferença, especialmente para quem está em fase de construção de carreira.

A comunidade cumpre exatamente o que se propõe: conectar pessoas, compartilhar conhecimento e fortalecer o ecossistema SAP local.

 

SAP Inside Track 2025

 

SAP Inside Track 2025

 

 

 

 

❓Que conselho você daria para quem está começando agora no ecossistema SAP e ainda não conhece a comunidade?


Aceite que o início faz parte do processo. No ecossistema SAP, ninguém começa sabendo tudo, e a comunidade existe justamente para isso. Participar da SAP Community Curitiba ajuda a entender melhor os caminhos possíveis, aprender com experiências reais e perceber que dúvidas são normais, principalmente no começo.
A comunidade é um espaço para perguntar, observar, trocar e evoluir no seu ritmo. Para quem está aprendendo SAP, isso faz toda a diferença.

Conecte-se comigo: https://www.linkedin.com/in/iron-lopes

 

🔁Convidado para continuar a série

 

Para continuar a série Somos SAP Community Curitiba, convido: @LeandroCSantos para compartilhar sua história e experiências na comunidade.

 

Além das perguntas da série:
1.  Quem sou eu na SAP Community Curitiba?
2.  O que mais me motiva a continuar fazendo parte do SAP Community Curitiba?
3.  Como fazer parte da comunidade impactou minha jornada profissional ou pessoal?

 

Deixo também a pergunta adicional:

 

Que conselho você daria para alguém que está começando agora no ecossistema SAP e ainda não conhece a comunidade?

 

Frase final

 

Sigo aprendendo, e a SAP Community tem sido uma grande ajuda nesse caminho. Agora, quem é o próximo a contar sua história?
 
 
#SomosSAPCommunityCWB
#SAPCommunityCWB
#SAPCommunity

entry.title: Re: From Hype to Reality: AI Discovery Workshop in SAP Serialization Life Sciences
entry.content:
Great summary, Anil—this workshop genuinely captured a pivotal moment for serialization. Having participated in the discovery sessions and co‑created several of the use cases with the broader group, what resonated most with me was how clearly the conversation shifted from managing compliance exceptions to anticipating and preventing them.

The predictive exception management and AI‑driven root cause analysis use cases we explored directly address the day‑to‑day friction teams face—manual triage, fragmented partner data, and late visibility into systemic issues. What’s exciting is that these aren’t theoretical concepts; they’re grounded in real serialization signals we already trust and operate on today.

I also appreciate  Amir from design team and all SAP Team. Also SAP AppHaus, which has a human‑centered approach. By anchoring the AI discussion in actual operator, quality, and compliance workflows, the outcomes felt both practical and adoptable—exactly what’s needed to move from pilots to scaled value.

I’d be very interested to hear your perspective as these use cases would have moved toward real‑world adoption—particularly how you see them shaping industry‑wide maturity in serialization, beyond individual programs. The potential impact on operational resilience, partner collaboration, and overall supply chain trust feels significant.

Looking forward to seeing how the momentum from this workshop translates into measurable outcomes across the ecosystem.

entry.title: KIT to ORDER - EWM without VAS
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a scenario for Kit to Order without VAS but using a Pack Station in EWM. I am little lost in the process and need your expert advice.

I have created a copy of standard KIT header and Item Category for the Sales Order Items to for kitting process. I have created 1 Sales Order with 1 Kit Header (YKIT item category) with quantity 1 and 1 Kit component (YKMP item category) with quantity 1.  No BOM is involved.   

In EWM, I again used a copy of standard Item Types and mapping is accordingly done.  Upon saving Sales Order in ERP, Delivery is created and distributed to EWM as per configuration. 

The header item in EWM is showing as not relevant for Goods Issue as per standard config in EWM.

For the Kit Item, I was able to pick and, then pack in the work station into an Ship HU from the Pick HU and completed loading and posted GI. Everything worked from EWM side.

Now, I have problem on ERP side, after GI in EWM, when I checked ERP side, Split delivery happening and it creates a new delivery with only Component for which GI happened and Mat doc is posted for only that. However, the old delivery is still open with the Kit header item  .

I was expecting there will be 521(dummy GR) movement and 601 for the header item and hence the delivery will be completed. I also maintained configuration in IMG>Logistics Execution >Service Parts Management (SPM)>Outbound Process (SPM) > Kit to Order>Set Goods Receipt Movement Type for Kit Headers as 521.

Is the assumption correct for Kit to Order process in ERP side?  And could anybody guide what and how should I proceed in closing the deliveries .

entry.title: Re: From Compliance to Confidence Mastering Global Pharmaceuticals Serialization with SAP
entry.content:
This is a powerful and timely articulation of why serialization matters far beyond compliance. When you step back and look at the numbers—patient harm, economic loss, and erosion of trust—it becomes clear that serialization isn’t just a regulatory construct; it’s a global public health imperative.

What resonates most for me is the framing of serialization as a living trust mechanism. With regulations like DSCSA and EU FMD, we’ve reached a point where every product scan, every EPCIS event, and every verification request becomes an active safeguard for patients. When solutions like SAP ATTP and SAP ICH are embedded directly into core manufacturing, logistics, and partner collaboration workflows, trust is no longer assumed—it’s continuously verified.

I also appreciate how clearly this connects operational reality with strategy. End‑to‑end serialization only delivers value when internal control (ATTP) and external collaboration (ICH) work together at scale. That’s where the industry truly moves from reactive checks to proactive risk prevention—especially as volumes, partners, and regulatory complexity continue to grow globally.

Perhaps most important is the shift in mindset this enables: compliance as a baseline, not the finish line. When serialization data is leveraged intelligently, it becomes a driver of visibility, resilience, and competitive differentiation—while ultimately serving the most critical stakeholder of all: the patient.

Curious to hear how others across the ecosystem are seeing this evolution—particularly where serialization has already moved the needle from “necessary overhead” to measurable business and public‑health impact.

entry.title: Re: Ensuring DSCSA Compliance Readiness: How SAP Supports the Life Sciences Supply Chain
entry.content:
Excellent breakdown, Anil. This clearly captures why 2025 is not just another milestone year for DSCSA, but the moment where interoperability turns into operational reality for the entire U.S. pharma supply chain.

What stands out is how the May 27 manufacturer milestone has effectively de‑risked the journey for downstream partners. With unit‑level traceability, EPCIS exchange, and VRS already operating at scale for the largest manufacturers, wholesalers and dispensers now have a proven blueprint to follow—both technically and operationally.

The emphasis on SAP ATTP and SAP ICH working together is spot on. Internal serialization control without external connectivity doesn’t deliver DSCSA outcomes, and external data exchange without strong internal governance doesn’t scale. That L4–L5 pairing is what enables real‑time verification, exception‑based operations, and audit readiness without overwhelming teams with manual effort.

As wholesalers approach August and dispensers prepare for November, the real test will be less about can we comply and more about how smoothly we can operate under compliance. Programs that treat DSCSA as an embedded business capability—rather than a bolt‑on obligation—are already seeing the benefits in speed, reliability, and trust across trading partners.

Curious to hear how you’re seeing wholesalers and dispensers leverage the manufacturer momentum—especially where early adoption is already translating into fewer exceptions, faster verification, and more resilient supply chain operations across the network.

entry.title: 1H 2026: Skills & Insights in SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding to Fuel New Hire Productivity from Day 1
entry.content:
With the 1H 2026 release, we have delivered new capabilities within SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding to improve how organizations attract and hire the best talent.  

Through customer influence on the Customer Community and innovative product enhancements, we are bringing your ideas to life and driving improvements across the recruiting experience.

To learn more watch the on-demand Q&A webinars: 1H 2026 Product Release Webinars and Recruiting & Onboarding 1H 2026 Customer Release Q&A. To hear more about the roadmap and broader view of our strategy, watch the 1H 2026 VIP Virtual Program for Strategic Roadmap Updates.    

All of the 1H 2026 release items can be found on the What’s New Viewer. These new features will be live in Production on May 15th, 2026.

This release brings enhanced capabilities to the SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding solution, enabling hiring teams to streamline key onboarding activities, improve offboarding, and accelerate hiring.

We are delivering several enhancements that help organizations prepare new hires and the business for success with data-driven onboarding analytics and holistic skills alignment.

Onboarding Insights in People Intelligence

People Intelligence capabilities in SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding helps organizations more easily identify hiring bottlenecks, leverage real-time onboarding metrics, and helps optimize efficiency, productivity, retention, and overall business impact. Onboarding insights provides a comprehensive view of an organization’s onboarding process that enables HR teams to monitor, improve, and ensure a smooth and efficient onboarding experience with clearer visibility into onboarding progress and outcomes.  

Skills Alignment with the Growth Portfolio

Aligning skills with the Growth Portfolio helps new hires better understand their strengths, connecting them to meaningful roles, and charting a clear path for development from day one. Personalized career, development, and learning recommendations help new hires in building the skills needed to meet organizational goals.

Continuously Delivering Enhancements     

With every release, we are excited to deliver these new innovations and continue our journey of providing even greater value to our customers. For full details, please check out the 1H 2026 Release Highlights Resources: 

  • 1H 2026 Release Highlights Brochure
  • 1H 2026 Release Highlights Video 
  • Product Release & Road Map Information

Learn more about SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding.

entry.title: How to get started with the RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench
entry.content:
Now a days, companies are actively exploring easier and more reliable methods to upgrade and migrate their systems to unlock the full value of SAP S/4HANA and cloud solutions. 

Many organizations want a simplified and guided approach that reduces complexity during system transitions. Transition projects often involve multiple technical steps, tools, and dependencies that must be carefully managed. 

The RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench helps safeguard transition projects by providing a single centralized entry point. It offers a highly automated and guided system transition procedure designed to streamline the entire process. The workbench combines all necessary technical activities and tools into one structured framework. This approach reduces manual effort and minimizes the risk of errors during system upgrades and migrations. 

By integrating different SAP software logistics tools, the workbench simplifies the overall transition experience. As a result, customers can move to SAP S/4HANA and the cloud more efficiently while ensuring a smooth and reliable transformation journey. 

This blog covers:  

  1. What is “Rise with SAP System Transition Workbench”? 
  2. Available approaches 
  3. Preparation activities on SAP ECS and customer side 
  4. How to access the "RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench" to run cockpits 
  5. Advantages 
  6. Usage restriction 
  7. Frequently asked questions 
  8. References  

What is the "RISE with SAP system transition workbench"? 

  • The "RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench" is the single-entry point for your RISE journey.  
  • The system transition workbench is provided for the purpose of streamlining and standardizing the transition paths to RISE. 
  • It is a combination of manual and automated step-by-step instructions organized in Cockpits to complete the process successfully. 
  • It brings together existing tooling as required for the selected transition approach. 
  • It helps with preparation and prerequisite activities, ensuring system readiness. 
  • It provides growing set of health and quality checks reflecting SAP Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS)'s standards for a smooth handover and reduced downtime. 

Available Approaches 
The RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench supports different ways for migrating systems. Each method comes with detailed documentation that explains the process. The documentation gives clear steps and guidance to handle specific migration needs. 

 Refer to SAP Help: Cockpits Supported by "RISE with SAP system transition workbench" 

 

Cockpits Supported by "RISE with SAP system transition workbench" 

  • SAP HANA Tenant Copy 

  • SAP HANA Backup and Recovery 

  • Homogeneous "Update and Copy" 

  • System Conversion: SUM 2.0 (DMO with System Move) , SUM 2.0 (DMOVE2S4) 

 How the "RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench" works  
With RISE for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, operating system (OS) access is typically restricted. During the transition to RISE, a virtual machine is provided to perform some tasks which requires OS-level access. We call it a Migration Virtual Machine (VM). The Migration VM allows you to create a temporary system (intermediate System) and perform your chosen transition approach. 

The "System Transition Workbench" runs on the Migration VM. 

The System Transition Workbench automatically downloads the required Software Logistics (SL) tools from the Software Download Service (SDS). 

It makes sure that the latest version of each tool is used and integrates them to work together during the process. 

The "System Transition Workbench" stores all created and necessary secrets in a password vault. For more information, see Security Features. 

During the preparation phase the "System Transition Workbench" requires an internet connection to install a temporary system (intermediate System) on the Migration VM.  

The PAS (primary application server) instance cannot start since it is not connected to a database yet. It is required for the postprocessing steps; there it gets started and connected to the target database to perform the final checks. A direct connection is required between the source and target database. 

Preparation activities on SAP ECS and customer side 

Preparatory actions to be performed by SAP ECS 

  • The SAP system transition workbench is installed by SAP ECS on a Migration VM. Once the Migration VM is ready, SAP ECS hands it over to the migration partner. 
  • Before installing the System Transition Workbench, the SAP Host Agent must be set up correctly with the right certificates. SAP ECS handles this setup. 

Note: Scenarios in the Early Adopter Care program are by default not included on the Migration VM. If you are part of such an Early Adopter Care program, you must ask SAP ECS to enable it on the VM. 

Actions to be performed by users 

  • The workbench requires access to the Internet, preconfigured by the user's proxy. 
  • Remote Function Call (RFC) 

 How to Access the "RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench" to Run Cockpits 

Access the https://<Migration VM name>:1129/lmsl/slocon/docs/index.html with which you can use the same <Migration VM name> for several procedures. Authenticate using the sapadm administrator user of the SAP Host Agent. 

Once you have logged in, you can see System Transition Workbench catalog with the available Cockpits. 

To select the required Cockpit, choose the Create button and follow the instructions provided. 

Advantages  

  • Smooth, secure fast transition into a cloud model.  
  • Safeguarded transition through step-by-step guidance covering all necessary steps, based on SAP best practices. 
  • Resilient transition through automation, reducing the risk of errors and allowing predictability of the execution. 
  • Ensured resulting system quality, measured via a growing set of health and quality checks relevant for all systems. 
  • Simplifies and clarifies migration approaches to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. 

Usage Restriction: 

  • Above mentioned scenario are permitted for SAP ECS internal operations as well as for qualified migration partners and SAP Services. 
  • Qualified migration partners must have earned at least the “Essential Level” designation for the  SAP Cloud ERP Private competency as visible in the SAP Partner Finder. 
  • SAP provides required automation tooling and configuration on the virtual machine for the time of migration. 
  • Any non-authorized software export of the automation tooling or its configuration is prohibited. 
  • Any usage beyond defined usage constraints is prohibited. 
  • The RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench assumes that the target system and component releases are under maintenance. Technical limitations and/or (general) restrictions might require additional preparation or an upgrade to newer releases of SAP S/4HANA related applications, add-ons, or other technical components. 
  • The RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench is currently not available for SAP National Security Services (SAP NS2) customers. 
  • Currently, the RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench does not support SAP HANA scale-out target systems. 
  • The System Conversion using 2.0 (DMOVE2S4) for SAP ECC system with MSSQL databases is currently not supported. 

Frequently asked questions: 

  • How to check the version of the SAP System Transition Workbench? 

Log in to the RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench with URL https://<Migration VM name>:1129/lmsl/slocon/docs/index.html.  

Click the "About" button in the top right corner of the UI.  

Always verify the latest version in the tool. Refer to SAP Note 3462243 - RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench .

 

  • Does the System Transition Workbench" require Internet access?  

Yes, it requires internet access. Make sure that the user's proxy settings have been correctly configured. The SAP ECS proxy can be used for this purpose. 

 

  • Can I run multiple migrations simultaneously using a single Migration VM?  

No, you cannot run multiple migrations simultaneously. 

 

  • How to request/update thee "System Transition Workbench"? 

Go to SAP For Me , Services and Support dashboard -->Service Catalogue -->open the service request Update Transition Workbench. 

You can request a fresh installation, upgrade to the latest version, or re-installation of the RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench on the migration server. 

 References : 

https://support.sap.com/en/tools/software-logistics-tools/rise-with-sap-system-transition-workbench.html?anchorId=section_867077864 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SLTOOLSET/940e4b48bd434bcb95db32940d08694f/cc75825f2d7049db96b6c60ec269aac6.html?version=SHIP 

https://me.sap.com/notes/3462243 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SLTOOLSET/940e4b48bd434bcb95db32940d08694f/1276a251b9e2402f8b1f644bea60c768.html?version=SHIP 

https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/transformation/btc/stw.html

 

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG team

entry.title: Re: Period Determination Issue in “Close Period for Mater...
entry.content:
Hello @hyik 

Thank you for your question.

The Close Period for Material Master Records/Allow Posting to Previous Period does not determine the current period by an MM specific SSCUI alone. The period you see there is based on the MM period control record, which is at company code level, while the mapping of calendar dates to fiscal period and fiscal year comes from the fiscal year variant that is assigned to the company code.

Here are references for more guidance

3535266 - Understanding the handling of Material Document Numbers. - SAP for Me

2833921 - Error Allowed Posting Periods raised in app Create Supplier Invoice - SAP for Me

3196920 - 'Close Periods' app fails with error 'Incorrect period in control rec' for a preceding year - SAP for Me

So, in your example (and to answer your question Which SSCUI configuration is responsible for controlling the period determination in this app?). The answer is there is no single one that does it alone. With fiscal year variant SZ running from 21st March to 20th March, the app showing 002/2025 as current period is expected if:

  1. Your company code uses fiscal year variant SZ, which defines how dates are translated into fiscal periods, and
  2. Your MM period control record has already been advanced so that your current MM posting period is period 002/2025.

See the following references for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3ea5a1e277894aaebae7baee564040ba/36d96dc344214d2b803fc1114db99bc5.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/497a54d1c7714de69a4b1fa3012ebfdd.html?locale=en-US

Question 2: Is it solely driven by the Fiscal Year Variant, or are there additional configurations (e.g. MM-related settings) that influence the current period?

Answer: It is solely driven by the fiscal year variant. The fiscal year variant defines the period calendar, but the current open MM period is controlled operationally by the material management period control record, which is advanced by the Close Period for MM Records app. You can find more details in KBA 3196920 and 3252771

The general configuration and control points in practice are:

  • First, the fiscal year variant assignment to the company code.
  • Second, the MM period control record.
  • Third, the backposting flag/allow previous periodic logic.

 

To summarise:

My recommendation is to open the Allow Posting to Previous Period app, confirm what the system stores for the company code. (Code on display screen are for references only)

If needed, use CDS view I_COMPANYCODEPERIOD to technically verify the same values. Then review the company code’s assigned fiscal year variant and its maintained year dependent periods. If the fiscal year variant is correct but the app still shows an unexpected current period, the issue is usually that the MM period control record has already been advanced or not yet advanced, rather than a missing SSCUI.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Strategies for Downtime-Optimized DMO : doDMO, doC, DMOVE2S4 for large databases
entry.content: No, DMOVE2S4 approach primarily incorporates memory-pipe based data migrations , thus it can be used with doDMO,doC and DMO only. DMO with system move (both serial & parallel mode) works on file rather than memory-pipes, hence DMOVE2S4  does not work with it.

entry.title: Re: public cloud 2602, application job catalog entry coul...
entry.content:
Hello @Test1175 

Thank you for your question.

For custom Application Job parameters in Public Cloud, assigning component type= ‘EBELN’ is not sufficient to automatically get F4 help. The application Job framework does not simply consume the classic DDIC search help of the data element for its parameter UI. Instead, value help must be provided through the supported Application Job value help mechanisms, such as a CDS based value help or a custom value help implementation.

Here are references for more guiding details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/creating-cds-value-help?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/implementing-value-helps?locale=en-US

So, the issue is not the absence of the edit button itself. The real issue is that the parameter currently has no supported application job value help definition behind it. Your GET_PARAMETERS implementation correctly defines the parameter, but the framework still needs a value help artifact that it can render.

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/working-with-application-job-catalogs?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/creating-cds-value-help?locale=en-US

So, the recommended next steps are:

  1. You should keep the parameter definition in GET_PARAMETERS as you already did.
  2. You should create a CDS value help for the purchase document field if you want a standard searchable F4 help.
  3. If you need custom logic, implement a custom value help for the application job parameter instead.
  4. Synchronize the catalog entry again after you have concluded the value help implementation.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Reimagining the Customer Journey with AI-Powered SAP Sales and Service Excellence
entry.content:
📌  Introduction

How modern enterprises are weaving together intelligent automation, real-time knowledge, and seamless cross-platform workflows to deliver frictionless customer experiences.

In today’s digital-first world, customers expect fast, personalized, and seamless service experiences. Traditional disconnected systems often lead to delays, inefficiencies, and poor customer satisfaction.

To address this, organizations are adopting intelligent, integrated solutions that connect sales, service, and backend operations. By leveraging platforms like SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and SAP Field Service Management, along with AI capabilities, businesses can deliver a unified and proactive customer journey.

This blog demonstrates an end-to-end process that integrates AI, automation, and enterprise applications to optimize customer experience from initiation to closure.

💼  Business Use Case

Consider a scenario where a customer encounters an issue with a product and begins by attempting to resolve it through a self-service portal. If the issue remains unresolved, a case is created and handled by a service agent, supported by intelligent recommendations and knowledge systems. As the interaction progresses, the situation may evolve into a potential sales opportunity, enabling the organization to propose a suitable solution or upgrade. A quote is then generated and shared with the customer, who can review and sign it digitally, ensuring a seamless and efficient agreement process.

Once confirmed, backend systems take over to process the order and initiate service fulfillment. This includes creating the necessary orders and coordinating with field service operations, where a technician is assigned for on-site support. The technician completes the required service, resolves the issue, and updates the system, leading to the closure of the case.

This end-to-end use case demonstrates how organizations can transform traditional support interactions into revenue-generating opportunities, while automating backend processes, improving response and resolution times, and delivering a truly seamless, cross-functional customer experience.

🏗️   Architecture

The solution integrates multiple systems across the customer journey:


Frontend & Experience Layer
SAP Build Apps
• Self-service capabilities for customers
Customer Engagement Layer
SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2
• Case Management • Opportunity Management • Quote Management • AI-assisted troubleshooting
AI & Knowledge Layer
eGain Knowledge Management
• Intelligent Q&A • Knowledge Base search • Similar case suggestions
Digital Signature Integration
DocuSign
• Seamless agreement processing • Digital contract signing
Backend ERP Layer
SAP S/4HANA Cloud
• Solution Order • Sales Order • Delivery • Billing
Field Service Layer
SAP Field Service Management
• Service Call • Technician Assignment • Field execution

⚙️  Key Capabilities and Business Benefits

  • 🤖AI-Driven Self-Service : Automated issue identification and resolution suggestions
  • 📚Intelligent Knowledge Management : Context-aware recommendations using knowledge base
  • 🔄End-to-End Process Integration : Seamless flow from case → opportunity → quote → order → service
  • ✍️Digital Contract Management : Faster approvals using DocuSign
  • 🔧Smart Field Service Execution : Automated technician assignment and scheduling

📋  Prerequisites

Before implementing this end-to-end solution, it’s important to have the right landscape, integrations, and development setup in place.

🔧  Core Systems

Ensure access to the following key solutions:

  • SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 for case, opportunity, and quote management
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for backend order processing and billing
  • SAP Field Service Management for service execution and technician handling

🔌  Integration Layer

  • Middleware or API-based integration using SAP BTP (recommended)
  • Connectivity with DocuSign for digital agreements
  • Integration with eGain Knowledge Management for knowledge and AI-driven recommendations

🧠 AI Enablement

  • Configured and structured knowledge base
  • AI services enabled for intelligent Q&A, recommendations, and case insights

👨‍💻Development & Extension Setup

  • Use CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) to build custom microservices, autoflow logic, and backend extensions on SAP BTP
  • Develop UI applications using SAP Fiori for a consistent and enterprise-ready user experience
  • Expose and consume APIs for seamless communication between frontend, AI services, and backend systems

The step journey, unpacked

The flow is built around four macro-phases — Initiate, Assess & Troubleshoot, Resolve, and Closure — each supported by a distinct set of SAP and partner tools.

Step 1: Self-service

The customer lands on a self-service portal before ever reaching a human. Intelligent Q&A, powered by eGain Knowledge Management, deflects simple queries instantly.

Step 2 : Case creation

When self-service can't resolve the issue, a case is created in SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2. AI surfaces similar past cases and searches the knowledge base automatically.

Step 3: Opportunity

A service case becomes a commercial opportunity. Agents can pivot from problem-solving mode to solution-selling without switching systems.

Step 4 : Quote + DocuSign

A quote is generated and sent for e-signature via DocuSign — all within the same workflow. No email attachments, no context switching.

 

Step 5 : Solution order

In our Scenario we are using Sales order and Service Order

Once signed, the solution order in S/4HANA  is created and downstream fulfilment is triggered automatically.

 

Step 6 : Sales order fulfilment

SAP S/4HANA Cloud handles the sales order, delivery, and billing — a direct integration that eliminates re-keying of data.

Step 7 : Service order

In parallel, a service order is raised for any physical work that needs to happen on-site.

Step 8 : Service order replication

The service order is replicated into SAP Field Service Management, keeping every system consistent without manual effort.

Step 9 : Service call

A service call is initiated in SAP FSM, formally opening the field engagement.

 

Step 10 : Technician assignment

The right technician is assigned based on availability, skills, and location — the field team arrives prepared and resolve this issue

🏁Conclusion

This end-to-end flow demonstrates how organizations can transform customer journeys using AI and integrated enterprise platforms. By connecting sales, service, and backend operations, businesses can deliver faster, smarter, and more efficient customer experiences.

entry.title: Re: Syncing Invoices (Accounts Receivable)/Bills (Account...
entry.content:
Good day @accounts_mergea0d5be 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your details, I am assuming by:

  • AR Invoices you mean customer invoices/billing documents
  • AP Bills you mean supplier invoices

Then your current assumption is only half right. For AP bills, Supplier Invoice is exposed as an OData V2 API.

See the following reference for more insights

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bb9f1469daf04bd894ab2167f8132a1a/d8b16ace9227447c8c66086bc045a937.html?locale=en-US

For AR Invoices, the standard API is not only OData V4. So, if your AR object is the posted billing document/customer invoice, your assumption that it is only available via OData V4 is not correct.

See the following reference for more insights

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/03c04db2a7434731b7fe21dca77440da/78da10ca6df542acb5926ac921af99e9.html?locale=en-US

On deletion, the more important point is the Lifecyle behaviour:

  • For billing documents, SAP provides documentation on Read, Cancel, GetPDF, and also provides Billing Document Events
  • For supplier invoices, SAP documents Create, Read, Release, Reverse, and also reference Supplier Invoice Events in the related help pages.

That means the standard integration pattern is to track cancellation/reversal, not hard deletion of posted financial documents.

So, my recommendation is:

  1. Do not design this around physical deletes first.
  2. Use the object specific APIs by domain. If you need AR customer invoices as billing documents, use the Billing Document-Read, Cancel, GetPDF API. If you need AP bills, use Supplier Invoice-Create, Read, Release, Revers.
  3. For change tracking, prefer events where available.
  4. Do not assume delta token support for these two APIs unless the specific API metadata confirms it.

To summarise:

  • Bills, Supplier Invoices --> yes, OData V2
  • Invoices, Billing Documents --> also available in OData V2, son not V4 only
  • Deletion handling --> design for cancel, reverse and events, not for hard delete replication unless the exact API explicitly.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Set Field Mandatory for Profitability Segment in Post...
entry.content:
Good day @Nhi_247 

Thank you for your question.

The validation rule based on Accounting: Market Segment does not exactly cover/fit your business requirement. Note that the Market Segment business context is only valid for cost or revenue accounts assigned to a profitability segment. For balance sheet accounts, this context is not applicable, while Journal Entry Item is the context that is valid for all accounts.

See the following reference for more insights.

2892205 - Substitution or Validation Using the Market Segment Business Context - SAP for Me

In addition, the Assign Profitability Segment screen is a reused Fiori interface that is used by multiple transactions. The reused and that the fields shown depend on the business process. That explains why your rule is also affecting other processes such as invoice or sales related transactions, instead of only blocking postings in Post General Journal Entries.

So, for the requirement to make Product and Ship-to Party Mandatory in Post General Journal Entries for selected G/L accounts, the next steps I would recommend is:

  1. Check whether the affected G/L accounts are cost, revenue accounts or balance sheet accounts in Manage G/L Account Master Data. If they are balance sheet accounts, the Market Segment context is not the right standard option. See KBA 2892205 for more details.   
  2. Review whether the business can redesign the control so that it is applied only where the standard context is valid.
  3. If the requirement is strictly limited to manual journal entry posting only, consider a process workaround or a redesign, because the profitability segment interface is reused across multiple apps and the validation can therefore affect other transactions as well.

In summary:

The current role does not meet the requirement because the Market Segment validation context is standard only for cost or revenue accounts assigned to a profitability segment, not for all account types. Also, Assign Profitability Segment screen is a reused interface across multiple transactions, so, you creatin a rule there can affect other business processes as well, not only Post General Journal Entries.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Why Bringing SAP to Agentic SDK via Regular MCP Server Is Not Good Enough
entry.content:
Thank you, @Mattias! Indeed, there is no shortcut to success 🙂

Provisioning data to Generative AI is a quick win for conversational scenarios and process automation. However, Create / Update / Delete actions still require a lot of control and dedicated effort to plug them in. The efforts are not huge, but with the current level of technology, we should avoid the uncontrolled consumption of SAP data management interfaces by Generative AI solutions to safeguard data quality in the productive environment.

entry.title: Somos SAP Community Curitiba – Luiz Souza
entry.content:
Este post faz parte da série “Somos SAP Community Curitiba”, criada para compartilhar as histórias das pessoas que ajudam a construir e fortalecer o ecossistema SAP na nossa cidade.

A ideia da série foi apresentada neste post de abertura:
  Introdução - Série da comunidade: Somos SAP Community Curitiba

A proposta é simples: responder algumas perguntas e contar um pouco da nossa jornada dentro da comunidade.

😎Quem sou eu na SAP Community Curitiba?
Sou Luiz Souza, Sou desenvolvedor raiz desde 2003 e atualmente focado em sap invovação e tecnologia e Clean Core. E também sou curador da SAP Community Curitiba, atuando na organização e condução de iniciativas que conectam profissionais, promovem aprendizado contínuo e fortalecem o ecossistema SAP local. Tenho um papel ativo na criação de experiências como eventos, encontros técnicos, sempre buscando gerar valor real para a comunidade.

🤔O que mais me motiva a continuar fazendo parte da SAP Community Curitiba?
O que mais me motiva é o impacto direto nas pessoas. Ver profissionais evoluindo, compartilhando conhecimento e se conectando através da comunidade é algo extremamente gratificante. Além disso, a troca constante com especialistas e a oportunidade de estar próximo das inovações da SAP, como BTP, Clean Core e IA, me impulsionam a continuar contribuindo.

💎Como fazer parte da comunidade impactou minha jornada profissional ou pessoal?
Fazer parte da comunidade teve um impacto significativo na minha jornada. Profissionalmente, fortaleceu minha posição como referência em temas como SAP BTP, extensão e integração, além de ampliar meu networking com profissionais do Brasil e do exterior. Pessoalmente, desenvolvi habilidades de comunicação, liderança e confiança — especialmente ao conduzir eventos, inclusive em inglês — e vivi experiências marcantes que elevaram meu nível como profissional e como pessoa.

🤠Que conselho você daria para alguém que está começando agora no ecossistema SAP e ainda não conhece a comunidade? Não desista dos seus sonhos, e se mantenha sempre desafiado!

📸Um momento especial na comunidade

Foram vários, mas vou deixar uma trajetória para dizer que vale cada esforço!

SAP Inside Track São Paulo - 2023 - Como participante


SAP CWB Xperience 2025 - Ministrando Hands-on


SAP Inside Track Curitiba 2025 - Como Apoiador e Palestrante!




Recentemente como Host de um  SAP Code Jam 2026 mais que especial, com figuras internacionais!


🌐Conecte-se comigo
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/luiz-carlos-de-souza-ab6a2212/ 

✨Convidados para continuar a série
Para continuar a série Somos SAP Community Curitiba, convido:  @Lpinheiro   @Babbingtonn  @denisjunior  para compartilharem suas histórias e experiências na comunidade.

Além das perguntas da série:
1️⃣Quem sou eu na SAP Community Curitiba?
2️⃣O que mais me motiva a continuar fazendo parte da SAP Community Curitiba?
3️⃣Como fazer parte da comunidade impactou minha jornada profissional ou pessoal?

Deixo também uma pergunta adicional:
❓ Como você se você vê na comunidade daqui 2 anos ?

✨Frase final
 Entrar na SAP Community é mais do que aprender tecnologia — é se conectar com pessoas que vão acelerar sua evolução, abrir portas e transformar sua carreira de dentro para fora.

#SomosSAPCommunityCWB
#SAPCommunityCWB
#SAPCommunity

entry.title: Re: Replicate terminated utility contracts - CDP - Utilities Accelerator
entry.content:
• Use the DRF BAdI for contract outbound and adjust the selection so terminated statuses are included. This is usually the simplest and cleanest fix.

  • If you need more control, create a small custom DRF outbound object that sends terminated contracts (or just the key fields CDP needs).

  • Keep the standard flow untouched if other systems rely on it, and handle terminated contracts in a separate replication stream.

If you can share whether CDP needs full contract details or just end‑of‑life attributes, it’s easier to recommend the best approach.

entry.title: Re: Open item grouping in automatic clearing FPMA
entry.content:
FPMA will always create one clearing document unless you add your own grouping logic. Event FQEVENT 591 is the right place. The field AZVDG_0591 isn’t configured anywhere in IMG — you fill it yourself in the event. Whatever value you put there becomes the grouping key.

So if you want one clearing document per payment document, your event 591 code needs to read the payment doc number and move it into AZVDG_0591. FPMA will then split the clearing runs accordingly.

If you want, share how you’re currently filling the event and I can point out where to adjust it

entry.title: Re: What is the better certification for an FLM and DMS Consultant?
entry.content:
SAP certifications that fit your role

  • SAP S/4HANA Asset Management (EAM) – useful if your FLM work touches maintenance, work orders, or technical objects.

  • SAP S/4HANA Sourcing & Procurement – helpful if your DMS processes integrate with purchasing or document workflows.

  • SAP Business Process Integration (TS410) – good foundation if you want broader S/4 knowledge.

  • SAP ECM/DMS‑related courses (S4DMS, PLM300) – these aren’t always formal certifications, but they’re the most relevant for deep DMS expertise.

entry.title: What's New in Succession Planning: SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 Release.
entry.content:
The 1H 2026 release brings meaningful updates to Succession Planning in SAP SuccessFactors. If you're an administrator, succession planner, or HR business partner working with talent pipelines, these changes are worth your attention. They tighten security, expand filtering capabilities, and give Talent Search a much-needed boost.

Let's walk through the four key changes and what they mean for your configuration and day-to-day operations.

1. Generic Object and Foundation Object Filters Now Work in Position Tile Live Slides

Reference: SCM-21218

If you've used Presentations to create Position Tile live slides, you may have noticed that Generic Object and Foundation Object filter options appeared in the UI but didn't actually display any values. That gap has been closed.

Starting with 1H 2026, users creating or editing Position Tile live slides in Presentations can view and select values for both Generic Object and Foundation Object filters. The filter options displayed in Presentations now mirror the options configured for the Position Tile View, bringing full parity between the two experiences.

What You Need to Do

Before this works, make sure two things are in place:

  1. You've added Generic Object and Foundation Object fields as searchable fields to the Position object.
  2. You've enabled the setting Include Generic Objects and Foundation Objects in filters in the Position Tile view under Admin Center > Succession Settings.

If both are already configured, the enhancement takes effect automatically. No migration, no new permissions. It simply fills a functional gap that's been there since the filters were introduced.

2. New Permission Control over Successor Nominations

Reference: SCM-21707 | Customer Idea: 279053

This is the most significant change in the 1H/2026. SAP has introduced a new child permission under the Succession Planning Permission that gives administrators granular control over who can manage successor nominations and, critically, who can be nominated.

The New Permission

A new permission has been added at User Permissions > Succession Planners > View/Add/Edit/Delete Successor Nominations. It sits as a child under the existing Succession Planning Permission. This permission controls who can manage successor nomination information.

The Target Population Setting

Paired with this permission is a new option in Admin Center > Nominations Setup: Apply target population restrictions to the View/Add/Edit/Delete Successor Nominations permission.

Here's how it works:

  • Default (option not selected): Succession planners with the existing Succession Planning Permission can nominate all active users in the system. This is the current behavior. Nothing changes unless you explicitly enable the new setting.
  • Option enabled: Succession planners must have both the Succession Planning Permission and the new View/Add/Edit/Delete Successor Nominations permission. Only users within the target population defined for the permission role can be nominated as successors.

Impact on the UI

When the target population restriction is active, the UI behavior changes in several areas:

  • Position card: Planners can only view successors within their permission role's target population. Successors outside this scope are hidden.

  • Talent card: Planners cannot view current nominations or nomination history for users outside the target population.

  • People Profile (latest or legacy): Nomination information for users outside the target population is hidden on the Nominations and Successors cards (latest) or the Current Nominations and Successors blocks (legacy).

Fig 1: Cards in the latest People Profile

Fig 2: Blocks in the legacy People Profile

  • Adding nominations: Whether initiated from Succession module pages or from other integrated modules, the nomination is blocked if the nominees fall outside the target population.

Why This Matters

This enhancement addresses a real compliance need. Organizations can now ensure that only individuals who have, for example, recorded their consent in employee data are eligible to be nominated. This is particularly relevant for privacy regulations where nomination without consent could be problematic.

Configuration Recommendation

If your organization does not need nomination restrictions, do nothing. Leave the new setting disabled, and existing behavior continues unchanged.

If you do need restrictions, SAP recommends the following sequence, starting in your Preview environment:

  1. Grant the View/Add/Edit/Delete Successor Nominations permission to your succession planner roles.
  2. Define an appropriate target population for those permission roles.
  3. Enable Apply target population restrictions in the Nominations Setup tool.

Test thoroughly before enabling in production.

3. Permission-Based Visibility of Performance Ratings in AI-Assisted Successor Insights

Reference: SCM-21577

If your organization uses the AI-assisted Successor Insights feature (available on position cards in the Succession Org Chart or Position Tile view), this change adds an important privacy layer.

What Changed

Previously, when a succession planner generated AI-assisted insights for nominees on a position card, the system always displayed the nominees' latest overall performance ratings as part of the analysis. There was no way to restrict this.

Now, only succession planners who hold the User Permissions > Employee Data > Background Information permission for the element <sysOverallPerformance> can view those ratings. The ratings are only visible for users within the planner's target population. For users outside the target population, their performance ratings are excluded from the AI analysis entirely.

Configuration Prerequisites

This feature requires:

  • An AI units license (contact your Account Executive for details).
  • The Assisted Successor Insights option enabled in Admin Center > AI Services Administration.

Required Permissions

  • User Permissions > AI Access > Assisted Successor Insights for access to the feature itself.
  • User Permissions > Employee Data > Background Information for the element <sysOverallPerformance> to see performance rating data within the target population.

This is a welcome enhancement for organizations that treat performance ratings as sensitive data. It ensures that AI-generated insights respect the same permission boundaries as the underlying data.

4. Custom Attributes in Talent Search

Reference: SCM-20791

Talent Search has historically been limited to skills and competencies as search criteria. With 1H 2026, that changes. Users can now add custom attributes as search options, opening up a much broader data set for talent decisions.

How It Works

The Add dialog in Talent Search now surfaces the 50 most recently created attribute types, provided each contains at least one attribute. Users can drill down into an attribute type to select specific attributes, or use the search bar to find attributes by name.

On the search results page, users can:

  • View the name and proficiency level of custom attributes for each employee.
  • Compare employees side by side using custom attribute data.
  • Select custom attributes for inclusion in the exported search results file.

One helpful detail: when reloading saved search criteria, inactive attributes are automatically removed from pre-filled filters to prevent errors.

Who Benefits

This expands the usefulness of Talent Search for managers, HR business partners, succession planners, and anyone involved in talent decisions. Instead of being limited to skills and competencies, you can now leverage the full breadth of attributes defined in your Talent Intelligence Hub.

Prerequisites

  • Your organization has migrated to Talent Intelligence Hub.
  • The attributes and their attribute types must have an active status.
  • In Talent Search Settings, the option Show Attributes is selected and a rating scale is chosen for All Attributes.

Required Permissions

  • Growth Portfolio > Attribute > View
  • Growth Portfolio > Attribute Type > View
  • Growth Portfolio > Proficiency Level Scale > View
  • Succession Planners > Talent Search Access
  • Talent Search Field > Ratings > Attribute

Summary and Recommendations

Here's a quick rundown of what to prioritize:


ChangeAction RequiredImpact
Generic/Foundation Object filters in PresentationsVerify existing configLow effort, fills a UI gap
New Nomination Permission ControlEvaluate organizational need; test in Preview firstHigh impact for compliance-sensitive orgs
Performance Rating Visibility in AI InsightsReview Background Information permissionsMedium impact for AI-enabled customers
Custom Attributes in Talent SearchVerify Talent Intelligence Hub migrationHigh value for mature talent processes

The nomination permission change deserves the most attention. If your organization has any privacy, consent, or compliance requirements around succession nominations, this is the feature to evaluate and test before your production release.

For the full release documentation, visit the SAP SuccessFactors What's New Viewer.

This post covers Succession Planning changes in the SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 release. For a complete list of all changes across modules, refer to the What's New Viewer on the SAP Help Portal.

entry.title: Re: SAC Chart fails to render when using dimension with t...
entry.content:
Dear NilsTollning,

Hope you are well. Thank you for your message.

To address this scenario effectively, it is recommended reviewing the following points to ensure a resolution:

1. Since this story is based on the live Datasphere Analytic Model, it is recommended to check whether this issue can also be reproduced in Datasphere Analytic Model preview. 

This can be checked by logging into the Datasphere system, accessing the Analytic Model that this SAC story connects to, and entering the Preview mode of this Analytic Model. In this Analytic Model Preview, please kindly add the same measures, dimensions as well as dimension attributes. Then, please kindly check whether the same behaviour can also be observed in this Datasphere Analytic Model Preview. If yes, this issue may need to be further investigated from the Datasphere perspective.

2. If this issue cannot be reproduced in Datasphere Analytic Model Preview, please kindly check which language has been set in SAC User Profile and Datasphere User Profile for this user who accesses both the Datasphere Analytic Model Preview and SAC story. Please kindly verify that the language settings for this user have been configured identically in both the SAC User Profile and the Datasphere User Profile. 

If the language settings in the User Profile for both SAC system and Datasphere system are already aligned, and the issue persists solely within the SAC story, further investigation into the design of the SAC story may be required.

Thank you for your time.

Best wishes,

Lena

entry.title: Re: S4 GR hard validations
entry.content:
@ALKER 

It seems to be a line item validation error. 

In order to solve your issue you have to maintain the subitem 241, 265 and 266  in your selection, or post a correction document with document type 01 ( Manual correction of universal journal (0C) ) that reclassifies data from above subitem  to another subitem that is contained in another selection.

It can be maintained in app Define Selections, go to Edit mode and add a new Subitem.

You can refer to the below note.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3540221

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

Yes, this is correct. As documented in the blog:  Compatibility Pack: How to check it in your system going forward?.
There is going to be one source of information for customers to check CP usage in their systems and it is your Simplification Item check!

Thus, we suspended the Compatibility Package Usage check in Earlywatch Alert. Please refer to the above blog to apply the latest version of the note 2399707, to check the Compatibility Package Usage on your S/4HANA system with report /SDF/RC_START_CHECK.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: How to treat Item 37 and 38 in 01/GTGT form for Vietn...
entry.content:
Good day @Hide94

Thank you for your question.

When you look at it from the SAP side, the relevant point is that tax box mapping is specific to each country and only the tax box structures SAP delivers for that country is available and supported in the relevant SSCUI. This also explains why you don’t see it in Configuration link you provided. The importance of the specific nature of tax box mapping activity of each country, what is supported for that country and what you can maintain there is documented in KBA 3465735. For Vietnam, VAT declaration (Form 01/GTGT) exists, and the configuration is done through the delivered setup for your localization content.

So, if in SSCUI 102011 you do not see tax boxes 37 and 38, that would mean SAP does not currently provide these two items as standard tax-box-mapped fields in that SSCUI for Vietnam. In other words, those items are part of the legal form, but they are not exposed in standard SAP as normal tax code to tax box mapping targets in the way that other VAT boxes are.

For that reason, I would not recommend:

  • Creating a new G/L account only for item 37 and 38
  • Creating a new tax code only for item 37 and 38
  • Inventing a custom tax box logic to force values into those fields.

Doing that would mix up transactional VAT determination with prior-period adjustment reporting, and this is not a correct legal representation of the two items. In practices they belong to the adjustment process for the VAT declaration, not to the ordinary current-period tax posting process.

A recommended SAP approach is for you to:

  1. Use SSCUI 103011 only for the Vietnam tax boxes that SAP actually delivers in its standard functionality.
  2. Not create artificial tax codes or G/L accounts just to fill items 37 and 38.
  3. Treat items 37 and 38 as previous-period VAT adjustment fields and handle them through your appropriate local VAT adjustment and filing process.
  4. If your business requires SAP to populate these items systematically from the system, you may raise an incident with SAP to better understand if the limitation is intentional to the Vietnam localization content or a gap that requires an enhancement request.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6ab33fdcdccc4be6bb7ce69e8e87fff4/fffab61a0eed458782458d0910fab732.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Revenue Recognition URR - Requested function ENT1 is...
entry.content:
Good day @IsmaelMateos 

Thank you for providing the details. It has helped in better understanding the issue you face.

First, for customer projects with Universal Revenue Recognitions, you need to maintain Amount to Be Billed and a Standalone Selling prices, so when you save the project, the system creates a sales order for service. Also, for multiple-element arrangements in customer projects, you must maintain the SSP field for each service POB relevant for bundling.

Therefore, based on the error pattern you shared, the issue is not that your team is using the wrong process. The issue is that the subsequent sales order created from the solution order is missing the pricing condition PSAM, so the system cannot derive a valid total SSP for the IFRS-relevant item. See KBA 3695042 for the exact symptom you are experiencing.

So, the key point is this:

  • ENT1 is not the root cause in your case.
  • It is the visible orchestration error that appears because the follow-up sales order cannot be created correctly.
  • The real issue is that the IFRS-relevant item, the system expects the PSAM pricing condition in the generated sales order, and that condition is not being derived, so the system treats the item as having 0 total SSP.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/29cf986299714386847f4d4c9bb86994/3f6d30bca32240d69bb9cf008aa5e23a.html?locale=en-US

In summary:

  • Your error is caused by the follow-up sales order pricing, not by the project billing screen itself.
  • KBA 3695042 describes your symptoms and highlights the cause to be a missing price condition PSAM, which then leads to total SSP =0 for the IFRS-relevant item.

As a suggested next step:

  1. Keep your project billing item setup as you already have done, without Amount to Be Billed and SSP maintained.
  2. Check the sales-order item pricing the system generates and confirm whether condition PSAM exists and is greater than zero.
  3. Review your pricing setup for product URR_FPPPRJ_1 and the relevant sales-order pricing procedure used by the orchestration.
  4. If you still have issue, raise an incident and reference KBA 3695042.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Regulatory Content Service vs 3rd Party Data Provider as alternate to Stewardship network
entry.content:
Hello

regarding

Ask:

My question is can SAP Regulatory Content Service can be used as standalone for both Process/Discrete Industry whose sole purpose is to run Compliance Check on Substance(Material Compliance) - (And there is no need for running any of the rule /rulesets).

I am not sure if you have the correct understanding of the new solution (based on best knowledge)

Step 1: Let us assume you system is up and running and any dara is maintained on any level (which will not be the case in most of the case).

Step 2: now you get a content update 

Step 3: now it can be that based in the new content the old data is not correct any more (we call this: teh secondara data epends on primary data)

Step 4: now you need to check the relevant data. Based on best knowledeg of the new version: you need now to "check" gain. Here for some cases we have no a rule set running as in EHS Classcic; but you have the same approach; my understanding is: you need so assess the data and release it again (There is a "version" content of data based on "content" update you need to understand carefully)

Step 5: and only after the assessmen and by doing so relase the new data: this new data will be used in the process

My understading is (but still need to do more research): you can do a kind of virtuell check using the the new system: you can do a kind of "calculation": if data A is change: what other objects are effected = you get a kind of "work list" of objects which need a review because primary data has changed

But still I am here on the learning track as well. 

IN a nutshell: IN EHS Classic you use the "EHS Expert server"option; in the new solution we have a different framework
